verses.text: The righteous perish and none considers that they are 'taken away from the evil to come,' entering into peace. Death can be a mercy. God sometimes removes His own before the storm, granting them rest.

The righteous perish and none considers that they are 'taken away from the evil to come,' entering into peace. Death can be a mercy. God sometimes removes His own before the storm, granting them rest.
He rebukes the children of sorcery and adultery who 'inflame yourselves with idols under every green tree.' Their idolatry is shameless. A people who forsake God sink into every degrading worship.
He rebukes the children of sorcery and adultery who 'inflame yourselves with idols under every green tree.' Their idolatry is shameless. A people who forsake God sink into every degrading worship.

He rebukes the children of sorcery and adultery who 'inflame yourselves with idols under every green tree.' Their idolatry is shameless. A people who forsake God sink into every degrading worship.

They set their bed on the high places, sent gifts to false gods, and wearied themselves in idolatry yet would not say it was hopeless. Sin exhausts and never satisfies. The idolater spends himself chasing what cannot save.

They set their bed on the high places, sent gifts to false gods, and wearied themselves in idolatry yet would not say it was hopeless. Sin exhausts and never satisfies. The idolater spends himself chasing what cannot save.

Of whom were they afraid, that they lied and forgot God? Their idols cannot deliver, 'but he that putteth his trust in me shall... inherit my holy mountain.' Trust is rewarded. Fear of men breeds falsehood, but trust in God secures the inheritance.

Of whom were they afraid, that they lied and forgot God? Their idols cannot deliver, 'but he that putteth his trust in me shall... inherit my holy mountain.' Trust is rewarded. Fear of men breeds falsehood, but trust in God secures the inheritance.

'Thus saith the high and lofty One... I dwell in the high and holy place, with him also that is of a contrite and humble spirit.' The exalted God stoops to the lowly. He revives the broken heart that seeks Him.

'Thus saith the high and lofty One... I dwell in the high and holy place, with him also that is of a contrite and humble spirit.' The exalted God stoops to the lowly. He revives the broken heart that seeks Him.

God will not contend for ever; He creates 'the fruit of the lips; Peace, peace to him that is far off, and to him that is near.' He heals the penitent. The God who chastens also speaks peace and restores comfort.

God will not contend for ever; He creates 'the fruit of the lips; Peace, peace to him that is far off, and to him that is near.' He heals the penitent. The God who chastens also speaks peace and restores comfort.
But the wicked are like 'the troubled sea... casting up mire and dirt'; 'there is no peace... to the wicked.' Sin yields no rest. The restless heart that refuses God finds only turmoil.
